messages.author: Tania C.
messages.caveat: Discord excerpt contains host/operator context without a direct proposal address.
messages.excerpt:
Hey @Host! Instead of relying on a single centralized server, all network data and inference metrics are hosted directly on the Hosts' nodes. This means the dashboard / explorer can connect to any Host’s node and fetch live network data straight from the source. Open the Hosts list: http://node2.gonka.ai:8000/v1/epochs/current/participants. Choose any active Host from that list. Copy and paste inference_url into your browser to load the dashboard. 
Here is also an external open source viewer: http://34.60.64.109

messages.text:
I couldn’t participate in this epoch again, and I need some help identifying the issue. I’m tagging the admins, especially @Gleb Morgachev, because something strange keeps happening.
Right before the epoch starts, my node shows an error saying it can’t reach the ML node health check. A moment later, it says the health check is fine again. Everything appears to be running normally, yet I still don’t get added to the mining list.
My setup used to work perfectly. I even configured iptables to block all unnecessary ports and only allow the required public ones, so I’m not sure what’s causing this. I also ran the PoC simulation before the epoch started, and everything passed.
Is there something wrong with my configuration, or could this be a bug?
My address is: gonka1f7d5j263cw6ukux38kg5gglup83aazctchmtp8
Could someone please guide me on how to fix this or point me in the right direction?

messages.author: Berlin
messages.caveat: Discord excerpt contains host/operator context without a direct proposal address.
messages.excerpt:
1. Confirm ML node HTTP health / model-up endpoint:
# from the host (where docker compose runs)
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:5050/health || true
# or, inside the compose network (via api container)
docker compose exec api curl -s -I http://inference:8080/v3.0.8/api/v1/inference/up || true
2. Inspect ML node (mlnode) logs (replace service name if different):
sudo docker compose logs --tail=200 join-mlnode-308-1 # or `mlnode-<id>` as shown in your compose ps
3. Check GPU memory / usage:
nvidia-smi
Look for per-GPU total memory (79 vs 80 GB) and current process usage / OOM errors.
4. API logs for node availability messages:
docker compose logs api --tail=200 | egre...
messages.replyText: Original message was deleted or could not be loaded.
messages.text:
1. Confirm ML node HTTP health / model-up endpoint:
# from the host (where docker compose runs)
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:5050/health || true
# or, inside the compose network (via api container)
docker compose exec api curl -s -I http://inference:8080/v3.0.8/api/v1/inference/up || true
2. Inspect ML node (mlnode) logs (replace service name if different):
sudo docker compose logs --tail=200 join-mlnode-308-1 # or `mlnode-<id>` as shown in your compose ps
3. Check GPU memory / usage:
nvidia-smi
Look for per-GPU total memory (79 vs 80 GB) and current process usage / OOM errors.
4. API logs for node availability messages:
docker compose logs api --tail=200 | egrep -i "Node does not have neededModel|Failed to check model status|unexpected status code|502|not available" (edited)

messages.text:
Добрый день, сегодня после PoC увидел свой адрес в списке валидаторов. http://34.60.64.109/?participant=gonka1jhnv39dmnfdg84mt4sa5qgya69djaq3n27pld8
Но! Jail Status = ACTIVE Health Status = Healthy а вот MLNodes node1 = STOPPED
Как это исправить? После того как произошел PoC я менял конфигурацию, так как в логах увидел ошибку типа ValueError: The model's max seq len (40960) is larger than the maximum number of tokens that can be stored in KV cache (25088). Try increasing gpu_memory_utilization or decreasing max_model_len when initializing the engine.
В docker-compose.mlnode.yml я прописал: 
services:
mlnode-308:
image: ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:3.0.10
hostname: mlnode-308
volumes:
- ${HF_HOME:-${HOME}/.cache}:/root/.cache
- ./node-config.json:/app/node-config.json:ro
environment:
- HF_HOME=/root/.cache
- VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASHINFER
- VLLM_MAX_MODEL_LEN=25000
- VLLM_GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION=0.95
deploy:
resources:
reservations:
devices:
- driver: nvidia
count: all
capabilities: [gpu]
ipc: host
command: uvicorn api.app:app --host=0.0.0.0 --port=8080
restart: always
В node-config.json : 
"models": {
"Qwen/Qwen3-32B-FP8": {
"args": [
"--tensor-parallel-size", "2",
"--max-model-len", "25000",
"--gpu-memory-utilization", "0.95"
]
}
Но после перезапуска всеравно вижу в логах все ту же ошибку ValueError: The model's max seq len (40960) is larger than the maximum number of tokens that can be stored in KV cache (25088). Try increasing gpu_memory_utilization or decreasing max_model_len when initializing the engine.
Будто новая конфигурация не хочет применяться и моя нода в статусе STOPPED вместо INFERENCE

Запустил Gonka на 8x 4090, модель "Qwen/Qwen3-32B-FP8" сервер проходил время PoC, но в итоге я так понял участником полноценным не стал, никакой награды, веса, отображения в списке участников и т.д.
А в логах api такое:
2025/11/20 08:28:49 INFO Dispatching reconciliation command subsystem=Nodes node_id=node1 target_status=INFERENCE target_poc_status=IDLE blockHeight=1372652
2025/11/20 08:28:49 INFO Node has governance models configured but not yet assigned to epoch, skipping inference start subsystem=Nodes node_id=node1

Вот это "INFO Node has governance models configured but not yet assigned to epoch, skipping inference start subsystem=Nodes node_id=node1" нормально? Все время без остановки и непонятно надо ли еще сутки ждать, изменится ли что-то. Или проблемы, что эта модель исключается? Или что-то еще искать

messages.text:
Всем привет!
Возникло несколько вопросов "от новичка", на которые код и документация не дали ответа.
1. Есть сайт с документацией и раздел про рекомендуемые модели и железо - https://gonka.ai/host/hardware-specifications/
2. Есть на сайте раздел с https://gonka.ai/host/quickstart/#proposed-hardware-configuration где есть два конфига, Large & Medium. Также есть список моделей в разделе http://node2.gonka.ai:8000/dashboard/gonka/inference где их есть 5 на выбор.
+ есть бенчмарки (спасибо, очень удобно, помогает)
Для теста был выбран конфиг 4x4090 Qwen/QwQ-32B, TP=4. Запущен, проверен, отдан в API ноду и там показывается как единственный доступный конфиг. Насколько я понимаю, в следующий PoC (через 20 минут примерно) это конфиг применится и встанет в работу.
Также я встречал рекомендации на тему "надо иметь в среднем 2 ML ноды, или больше".
Вопросы, которые у меня возникли при этом:
1. Кому верить по конфигам, количеству и списку моделей? Коду, документации или рантайму?
2. в логи после запуска сыпятся запросы на валидацию ноды, до наступления PoC:
2025/12/01 12:35:11 INFO Inferences to sample subsystem=Validation ids="[{InferenceId:hfpNH7vLgFYX5/TsP9rJXAdOO9x1nUdmN8IcZcWAQx9Ur1eWKeGc+ycq3h94v9YGkunYwHzTJvC2lepJJcrE7w== ExecutorId:gonka18ee58ff309k3fv2dn9f8zfa9q94huk4ue9y7t4}]"
2025/12/01 12:35:11 INFO Inferences to validate subsystem=Validation inferences=[]
2025/12/01 12:35:16 INFO nodeAvailable. Node is not administratively enabled subsystem=Nodes nodeId=vai-4x4090-node1 adminState="{Enabled:true Epoch:0}"
2025/12/01 12:35:16 INFO Node not available subsystem=Nodes node_id=vai-4x4090-node1 reason="Node does not have model Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8"
А что это такое? В запросе на валидацию идет самый жирный по железу конфиг.
Будет ли это влиять в будущем?

messages.author: DEMIAN
messages.caveat: Discord excerpt is searchable context.
messages.excerpt:
"The problem was using the -p flag on containers.
It turns out that Docker makes changes directly on your iptables, which are not shown with ufw status.
Possible solutions are:
Stop using the -p flag. Use docker linking or docker networks instead.
Bind containers locally so they are not exposed outside your machine:
docker run -p 127.0.0.1:8080:8080 ...
If you insist on using the -p flag, tell docker not to touch your iptables by disabling them in /etc/docker/daemon.json and restarting:
{ ""iptables"" : false }
"
messages.text:
"The problem was using the -p flag on containers.
It turns out that Docker makes changes directly on your iptables, which are not shown with ufw status.
Possible solutions are:
Stop using the -p flag. Use docker linking or docker networks instead.
Bind containers locally so they are not exposed outside your machine:
docker run -p 127.0.0.1:8080:8080 ...
If you insist on using the -p flag, tell docker not to touch your iptables by disabling them in /etc/docker/daemon.json and restarting:
{ ""iptables"" : false }
"

messages.author: Gleb Morgachev
messages.caveat: Discord excerpt contains host/operator context without a direct proposal address.
messages.excerpt:
Hi! We have several nodes jailed and ready to unjail
What does it mean? If validator hasn't participate in signing blocks for 50 blocks in a row - it becomes jailed for a short period of time (about 15 min). In our system, it doesn't prevent getting the reward for inference if there were not issues with that detected. So, the reward is not lost
After short period (15 min currently), nodes can be unjailed back
You should use cold key to set unjail transactions:
export NODE_URL=http://47.236.19.22:18000
./inferenced tx slashing unjail \
--from <cold_key_name> \
--keyring-backend file \
--chain-id gonka-mainnet \
--gas auto \
--gas-adjustment 1.5 \
--fees 200000ngonka \
--node $NODE_URL/chain-...
messages.text:
Hi! We have several nodes jailed and ready to unjail
What does it mean? If validator hasn't participate in signing blocks for 50 blocks in a row - it becomes jailed for a short period of time (about 15 min). In our system, it doesn't prevent getting the reward for inference if there were not issues with that detected. So, the reward is not lost
After short period (15 min currently), nodes can be unjailed back
You should use cold key to set unjail transactions:
export NODE_URL=http://47.236.19.22:18000
./inferenced tx slashing unjail \
--from <cold_key_name> \
--keyring-backend file \
--chain-id gonka-mainnet \
--gas auto \
--gas-adjustment 1.5 \
--fees 200000ngonka \
--node $NODE_URL/chain-rpc/
Then to check if node was unjailed:
./inferenced query staking delegator-validators \
<cold_key_addr> \
--node $NODE_URL/chain-rpc/
When node is jailed it shows jailed: true
The full list of node to unjail in the next message

messages.text:
21.11.2025 02:36I couldn’t participate in this epoch again, and I need some help identifying the issue. I’m tagging the admins, @Gleb Morgachev @Tania C. because something strange keeps happening.
Right before the epoch starts, my node shows an error saying it can’t reach the ML node health check. A moment later, it says the health check is fine again. Everything appears to be running normally, yet I still don’t get added to the mining list.
My setup used to work perfectly. I even configured iptables to block all unnecessary ports and only allow the required public ones, so I’m not sure what’s causing this. I also ran the PoC simulation before the epoch started, and everything passed.
Is there something wrong with my configuration, or could this be a bug?
My address is: gonka1f7d5j263cw6ukux38kg5gglup83aazctchmtp8
Could someone please guide me on how to fix this or point me in the right direction?
I had also checked the public ports externally to make sure they are correctly configured

Greetings to all!
I have analyzed my own and other people's nodes, and I cannot understand why I have such a small weight and few awards.
My Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct node with 2x NVIDIA L4 | 22GB, that is, a total of 44GB, compared with similar nodes
https://www.gonka.cloud/?participant=gonka1rmr4x8lyw76gzuyzta68gj965zfa00gzh2aszz - my node , the average weight is from 65-79 and the reward is 8-9
https://www.gonka.cloud/?participant=gonka15xr92pc7v0jvq5aqwqt43hpvvpvs3q5229lrmh - there is 44GB memory, weight 203
https://www.gonka.cloud/?participant=gonka1ddswmmmn38esxegjf6qw36mt4aqyw6etvysy5x - 40GB memory, 170G weight
Explain, who can possibly understand what kind of pricnip all these calcu...
messages.text:
Greetings to all!
I have analyzed my own and other people's nodes, and I cannot understand why I have such a small weight and few awards.
My Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct node with 2x NVIDIA L4 | 22GB, that is, a total of 44GB, compared with similar nodes
https://www.gonka.cloud/?participant=gonka1rmr4x8lyw76gzuyzta68gj965zfa00gzh2aszz - my node , the average weight is from 65-79 and the reward is 8-9
https://www.gonka.cloud/?participant=gonka15xr92pc7v0jvq5aqwqt43hpvvpvs3q5229lrmh - there is 44GB memory, weight 203
https://www.gonka.cloud/?participant=gonka1ddswmmmn38esxegjf6qw36mt4aqyw6etvysy5x - 40GB memory, 170G weight
Explain, who can possibly understand what kind of pricnip all these calculations are going on and how can you influence this?
Is this node-config.json, maybe I have incorrect settings?
[
{
"id": "node1",
"host": "inference",
"inference_port": 5000,
"poc_port": 8080,
"max_concurrent": 500,
"models": {
"Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct": {
"args": [
"--quantization",
"fp8",
"--gpu-memory-utilization",
"0.9"
]
}
}
}
]

volumes:
${HF_HOME:-${HOME}/.cache}:/root/.cache environment:
HF_HOME=/root/.cacheVLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASHINFER deploy:

messages.excerpt:
I did a clean re-init, but I am getting a validation error with the seed node (195.242.13.239).
Error: wrong Block.Header.AppHash
My Local Genesis (downloaded from node2): Expects FFEBEF...
The Seed Node (195.242.13.239): Sends 9A3FAFD...
Because of this, the peer is stopping, and I am stuck at height 1.
Which genesis file is the correct one for the current network? Should I be using the one matching 9A3FAFD?

messages.text:
@everyone 
How to edit Host public info
This guide shows how to update your Host/Validator profile with the human-readable name, website, and avatar/profile identity so that explorers display correct information.
PrerequisitesYou must be the operator of the Host/Validator (you hold the operator key).Your node must be running and connected to the network.If you want a verified avatar, have an identity service (for example, Keybase.
## Fields / ParametersHere are the only fields you can set or edit.
Field: Moniker
Flag: --new-
Purpose / What is displayed: The public name of your Host/Validator, shown in explorers.
Field: Website
Flag: --website
Purpose / What is displayed: Link to your Host/Validator’s website or project page. Displayed so delegators can learn more.
Field: Identity
Flag: --identity
Purpose / What is displayed: Typically used to provide a verification/proof identity (e.g., Keybase, which many explorers use to fetch your avatar/logo. You need to download the application from the website to generate a PRP key to fetch your logo.
Step-by-step guideRun the following command if you don’t have a PGP key yet.
keybase pgp gen
You will be prompted:
Push an encrypted copy of your new secret key to the Keybase.io server? Enter Y for Yes.When exporting to the GnuPG keychain, encrypt private keys with a passphrase? Enter Y for Yes and N for NoRun the following command if you have an existing PGP key, import it into Keybase.
keybase pgp select
Open the Keybase app. Enter your real name, which will be publicly visible in explorer.
Name your device (it can not be changed in the future).
Click on the avatar in the top left corner. Click “View/edit profile”.
Copy your 64-bit PGP. You will need it for --identity flag in the command below.
Update your node info
Run this command to edit your Host/Validator information. Make sure to replace cold-key-name, YourNewValidatorName, https://updated.website, and PGP-64-ID with your own values.
./inferenced tx staking edit-validator \
--chain-id="gonka-mainnet" \
--from <cold-key-name> \
--new-moniker <YourNewValidatorName> \
--website <https://updated.website> \
--identity <PGP-64-ID> \
--keyring-backend file \
--node <NODE_URL>/chain-rpc/ \
--yes
Once you send the transaction, wait for it to be included in a block and confirmed by the network. Check your Host/Validator info:
./inferenced query staking delegator-validators \
<cold-key-address> \
--node <NODE_URL>/chain-rpc/
This should show the updated moniker, website, and identity.
Example output
...
validators:
- commission:
commission_rates:
max_change_rate: "0.010000000000000000"
max_rate: "0.200000000000000000"
rate: "0.100000000000000000"
update_time: "2025-08-27T23:56:24.580275479Z"
consensus_pubkey:
type: tendermint/PubKeyEd25519
value: XMTuK2T6ojmAfcDzv5scXtl9QkgYaqwAnnyo7BdLKS4=
delegator_shares: "186.000000000000000000"
description:
details: Created after Proof of Compute
identity: 673C81B66A67ED67
moniker: gonkavaloper18lluv53n4h9z34qu20vxcvypgdkhsg6n02fcaq
website: https://gonka.ai
Wait for the explorer to index the new data (may take several minutes to hours). Then check your explorer — your name, website, and avatar should appear.

messages.text:
Hi,
I'm having an issue where my node has been registered on-chain but has never been included in any epoch group since registration (epochs 199–202).
Diagnostics I've run:
inference-participant query → returns pubkey correctly (AmRGjOq6UGYSrYjGMGjtGvjc2kIzc087GKekiTclygML)
excluded-participants → my address is not listed
get-participant-current-stats → returns {} (empty, across epochs 199–202)
v1/epochs/current/participants → my address is never included
authz grants → 27 grants confirmed
Cold address: gonka10dmx0u542nttu4s4xn0n2t5yy4ctgwtuht920q
The node is fully set up per the documentation — all settings verified, authz grants are complete, and the participant is registered on-chain. However, it has never once been assigned to an epoch group.
Given that the participant exists on-chain but has zero stats and has never appeared in any epoch's participant list, I suspect there may be a bug in the new participant bootstrap logic — where newly registered participants are never being picked up for their first epoch assignment. Could you please investigate whether this is a known issue or a bug?

messages.excerpt:
Hi! The PR for on-chain upgrade v0.2.4 which we had couple days ago, also introduced new version of MLNode which makes work with GPUs more stable and enables automatic model weights downloading
To upgrade mlnode to the latest v3.0.10 version:
1. Go to gonka/deploy/join 
2. Pull latest changes from main branch: git remote prune origin && git checkout . && git checkout main && git pull 
3. Restart container using the new image v3.0.10:
docker compose -f docker-compose.mlnode.yml pull && docker compose -f docker-compose.mlnode.yml up -d
Please do that update at least 500 blocks before start of the PoC phase or 500 block after it's end! 
The next PoC start at block 980988 and ends at 981293 
ht...
messages.text:
Hi! The PR for on-chain upgrade v0.2.4 which we had couple days ago, also introduced new version of MLNode which makes work with GPUs more stable and enables automatic model weights downloading
To upgrade mlnode to the latest v3.0.10 version:
1. Go to gonka/deploy/join 
2. Pull latest changes from main branch: git remote prune origin && git checkout . && git checkout main && git pull 
3. Restart container using the new image v3.0.10:
docker compose -f docker-compose.mlnode.yml pull && docker compose -f docker-compose.mlnode.yml up -d
Please do that update at least 500 blocks before start of the PoC phase or 500 block after it's end! 
The next PoC start at block 980988 and ends at 981293 
http://34.41.15.126/?page=timeline
If you use any custom way to deploy MLNode, please make sure HF_HUB_OFFLINE=true is removed from env variables (edited)

messages.text:
Добрый день, сегодня после PoC увидел свой адрес в списке валидаторов. http://34.60.64.109/?participant=gonka1jhnv39dmnfdg84mt4sa5qgya69djaq3n27pld8
Но! Jail Status = ACTIVE Health Status = Healthy а вот MLNodes node1 = STOPPED
Как это исправить? После того как произошел PoC я менял конфигурацию, так как в логах увидел ошибку типа ValueError: The model's max seq len (40960) is larger than the maximum number of tokens that can be stored in KV cache (25088). Try increasing gpu_memory_utilization or decreasing max_model_len when initializing the engine.
В docker-compose.mlnode.yml я прописал: 
services:
mlnode-308:
image: ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:3.0.10
hostname: mlnode-308
volumes:
${HF_HOME:-${HOME}/.cache}:/root/.cache./node-config.json:/app/node-config.json:ro environment:
HF_HOME=/root/.cacheVLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASHINFERVLLM_MAX_MODEL_LEN=25000VLLM_GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION=0.95deploy:
resources:
reservations:
devices:
driver: nvidia
count: all
capabilities: [gpu] ipc: host
command: uvicorn api.app:app --host=0.0.0.0 --port=8080
restart: always
В node-config.json :
"models": {
"Qwen/Qwen3-32B-FP8": {
"args": [
"--tensor-parallel-size", "2",
"--max-model-len", "25000",
"--gpu-memory-utilization", "0.95"
]
}
Но после перезапуска всеравно вижу в логах все ту же ошибку ValueError: The model's max seq len (40960) is larger than the maximum number of tokens that can be stored in KV cache (25088). Try increasing gpu_memory_utilization or decreasing max_model_len when initializing the engine.
Будто новая конфигурация не хочет применяться и моя нода в статусе STOPPED вместо INFERENCE (edited)

• All containers running healthy

• Node...
messages.text:
Hi everyone, need help with a catch-22 situation!
My node has been stuck since installation and can't activate.
Participant: gonka153urk3scd5axt5qwfvst6m7vvqds4ntm682z5t
THE PROBLEM (catch-22):
• To get hardware registered → need to complete PoC
• To receive PoC assignments → need weight > 0 
• To get weight > 0 → need hardware registered
CURRENT STATE:
• hardware: null (not registered)
• status: STOPPED (4)
• weight: -1 (not participating)
• epochs_completed: 2 (but GNK balance: 0 - never actually worked)
WHAT I'VE DONE:
• Fixed POC_CALLBACK_URL from http://api:9100 to http://212.93.107.107:40165
• Restarted API container
• All containers running healthy
• GPU detected: 2x RTX 3090
• Node synced with blockchain
• Model loaded: Qwen/Qwen3-32B-FP8
THE ISSUE:
Node never received PoC assignments due to wrong POC_CALLBACK_URL initially.
Now it's fixed, but the node seems stuck in "inactive" state.
QUESTION:
Will the network give my node a chance in the next epoch, or does weight: -1 block automatic activation? Do I need manual intervention to break this cycle?
Hardware: 2x NVIDIA RTX 3090 24GB

messages.excerpt:
Hello!
My address has acquired the “Jailed” status.
We fixed everything on the server (8×H100), but it’s still stuck in Jailed status.
Address: gonka13wtjcm0p79257ueldssvj2dhc6qh9w4u5fusn4
I restored the cold key. On my local machine, I enter the following command:
./inferenced \
tx slashing unjail \
--from gonka-sergei-koniakov2 \
--keyring-backend file \
--gas auto \
--chain-id gonka-mainnet \
--home ./gonka-sergei-koniakov2 \
--fees 200000ngonka \
--node http://node2.gonka.ai:26657
It returns the error:
failed to convert address field to address: gonka-sergei-koniakov2.info: key not found [gonka-ai/cosmos-sdk@v0.53.3-ps13/crypto/keyring/keyring.go:566]
Even though the keys are in place....

iptables -I DOCKER-USER 1 -p tcp -s 127.0.0.1 --dport 9200 -j ACCEPT
iptables -I DOCKER-USER 2 -p tcp --dport 9200 -j DROP (edited)

messages.excerpt:
Hello to everyone
I already got a reward in a 87 epoch. But today I see only "No MLNodes configured" at https://tracker.gonka.hyperfusion.io/?participant=gonka1x2qapwtlprmlcxrdtvrskswuznn8v29r4se5tc
I have checked twice all my staff. All servers are alive. No connection errors were happened. Logs a clear. Health Checks are green. 
What is the problem with my node? How to debug? And how to fix?

messages.author: key_b.official
messages.caveat: Discord excerpt contains an address and operator/identity wording; requires corroboration.
messages.text:
@Tania C. @Gleb Morgachev I couldn’t participate in this epoch again, and I need some help identifying the issue. I’m tagging the admins, especially @Gleb Morgachev, because something strange keeps happening.
Right before the epoch starts, my node shows an error saying it can’t reach the ML node health check. A moment later, it says the health check is fine again. Everything appears to be running normally, yet I still don’t get added to the mining list.
My setup used to work perfectly. I even configured iptables to block all unnecessary ports and only allow the required public ones, so I’m not sure what’s causing this. I also ran the PoC simulation before the epoch started, and everything passed.
Is there something wrong with my configuration, or could this be a bug?
My address is: gonka1f7d5j263cw6ukux38kg5gglup83aazctchmtp8
Could someone please guide me on how to fix this or point me in the right direction?
I had also checked the public ports externally to make sure they are correctly configured (edited)

messages.text:
Hello, team! I need a little help with creating an account on the mainnet for the gonka-openai SDK test.
The situation in a nutshell:
I had a Keplr account connected to http://node2.gonka.ai:8000Address: gonka1dfm8vqcd5e2cgsznhn6x692ggr9vzz9kwe3r0mExported the seed phrase (12 words), tried to recover the private key in all possible ways:
• m/44'/118'/0'/0/0 → secp256k1
• m/44'/60'/0'/0/0 → Ethereum-path• iterating through indexes 0-15
• cosmospy, gonkapy, bech32 + ecdsa manually
→ the signature is always invalid signature (401), although the address matches perfectly
Then I decided not to worry and created a completely new random account through Python + secp256k1.:
Current address: gonka12s4z5m8zazct8acn68wg9xztaz0juvyd43ayfq
The private key (without 0x) is already set to GONKA_PRIVATE_KEY, the SDK sees the address, the signature is valid, and the node accepts requests.
The only problem remains: the account is new → it is not on the chain yet → any node (changes automatically: 85.234..., 93.119..., node2...) responds
500 Internal Server Error → account not found: key not found
The official faucet https://gonka.ai/faucet It's 404 now, and I haven't found any other public faucets.
I really want to finally test the Qwen3-235B in the Grace Period, I've already set everything up on the VPS, all that remains is to create an account on the chain.
Please send a couple of GNKS to the address (at least 0.1–1 is enough) for the account to appear and I could make the first information request.
Address again:
gonka12s4z5m8zazct8acn68wg9xztaz0juvyd43ayfq
Thank you so much in advance! I am ready to share the full guide "how to upgrade your Gonka client from scratch to a VPS" for the community, if necessary.

messages.text:
Subject: Participant not registered despite node fully synced and account active
Message:
Hello,
My node is fully synced (catching_up = false), TMKMS is connected, ML node is running normally, and my account now exists on-chain. However, the endpoint /participant/check still returns:
{"error": "Not Found"}
Here are the details:
Node address: gonka1yvlhxn4594m0xzwcw60stu2qndn70m63tq99kg
The account is already created on-chain:
type: cosmos-sdk/BaseAccount
account_number: "5615"
ML node loads the model correctly (all GPUs allocated, vLLM running)
DAPI processes block events normally (NewBlock, inference_finished)
No more errors like account ... not found
It looks like the participant record has not yet been created or activated on the network side.
Question:
When should I expect the participant to be registered and /participant/check to return a valid participant status?
Is there anything else required on my side, or is this handled automatically by the network?
Thank you!

But today Claim status says NO in Gonka Tracker. I checked logs and it shows that rewards was assigned buy didn`t claimed:

But it still shows status NO.
Please help me before 105 epoch starts so i will not loose my rewards.

PUBLIC_URL=http://localhost:8080
P2P_EXTERNAL_ADDRESS=tcp://192.168.1.202:5000
ACCOUNT_PUBKEY=...
messages.text:
Hi! I need help with my Gonka node on Windows (Docker).
The node containers start, but the main node keeps restarting. 
In logs I get the errors:
“Environment variable SEED_NODE_RPC_URL is required”
“Environment variable KEY_NAME is required”
“KMS connection failed”
“Could not resolve node2/node3 host (3: host not found)”
“Error with private validator socket client: can’t get pubkey”
My .env file was missing SEED_NODE_RPC_URL and SEED_NODE_P2P_URL. 
I added them, but the node still restarts and doesn’t sync.
Here is my full .env:
KEY_NAME=mykey
KEYRING_PASSWORD=1234
API_PORT=8080
API_SSL_PORT=8443
PUBLIC_URL=http://localhost:8080
P2P_EXTERNAL_ADDRESS=tcp://192.168.1.202:5000
ACCOUNT_PUBKEY=Vm7SPmsTn1CGUvtKQwKabJPe1VUKiOVrn1W9Ir7o
CHAIN_ID=gonka-mainnet
COIN_DENOM=igonka
STATE_DIR=/root/.inference
INIT_ONLY=false
SNAPSHOT_INTERVAL=1000
SNAPSHOT_KEEP_RECENT=5
PRUNING_INTERVAL=2000
SEED_NODE_RPC_URL=http://node2.gonka.ai:26657
SEED_NODE_P2P_URL=node2.gonka.ai:26656
SEED_NODE_APP_URL=http://node2.gonka.ai:5000
DAPI_RPC_SERVER_URL=http://node2.gonka.ai:26657
DAPI_RPC_SERVER_URL_2=http://node3.gonka.ai:26657
DAPI_POC_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:8080/poc
PORT=8080
INFERENCE_PORT=5050
KEYRING_BACKEND=test
Could you please confirm if these seed URLs are correct for mainnet 
and why the node still fails to start state-sync?
Thanks!

messages.excerpt:
Hi everyone!
Maybe I found a possible bug in ml node api.watcher.
After message about vLLM server timeout
2025-12-11 08:10:10,863 - api.watcher - CRITICAL - Manager InferenceManager has been unhealthy 3 times in a row. Shutting down the application.
/usr/lib/python3.12/multiprocessing/resource_tracker.py:279: UserWarning: resource_tracker: There appear to be 2 leaked semaphore objects to clean up at shutdown
server process was restarted completely:
INFO: Started server process [320182]
INFO: Waiting for application startup.
2025-12-11 08:10:49,968 - api.models.manager - INFO - ModelManager initialized with cache_dir: /root/.cache/hub, stall_timeout: 600.0s (10.0 min)
2025-12-11 08:10:50,067...
messages.text:
Hi everyone!
Maybe I found a possible bug in ml node api.watcher.
After message about vLLM server timeout
2025-12-11 08:10:10,863 - api.watcher - CRITICAL - Manager InferenceManager has been unhealthy 3 times in a row. Shutting down the application.
/usr/lib/python3.12/multiprocessing/resource_tracker.py:279: UserWarning: resource_tracker: There appear to be 2 leaked semaphore objects to clean up at shutdown
server process was restarted completely:
INFO: Started server process [320182]
INFO: Waiting for application startup.
2025-12-11 08:10:49,968 - api.models.manager - INFO - ModelManager initialized with cache_dir: /root/.cache/hub, stall_timeout: 600.0s (10.0 min)
2025-12-11 08:10:50,067 - api.proxy - INFO - vLLM proxy started
2025-12-11 08:10:50,067 - api.proxy - INFO - Health check loop started, checking every 2.0 seconds
INFO: Application startup complete.
INFO: Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8080 (Press CTRL+C to quit)
And finally - the vLLM processes was leaked in running state. 
As result - after restart I get a bunch on OOM errors and only ps + kill commands running manually helps me.

I exexuted curl http://127.0.0.1:9200/admin/v1/setup/report | jq
Summary output is:
"summary": {
"total_checks": 11,
"passed_checks": 9,
"failed_checks": 2,
"unavailable_checks": 0,
"issues": [
"Participant is NOT active in epoch 110",
"Validator is NOT in consensus validator set (may need to register or unjail)"
],
"recommendations": [
"Check PoC participation and ensure node is properly registered"
]
}
Logs of all containers are OK. Output of nvidia-smi shows that memory of my GPUs filled. What next? I need to wait PoC?

messages.author: Tania C.
messages.caveat: Discord excerpt contains an address and operator/identity wording; requires corroboration.
messages.excerpt:
Hi @Host , check whether your node is on the list. If it is, you have not received a reward for the previous epoch (110). Check your node! If a reward was assigned but not claimed, make sure your node claims it before the next epoch starts.
IMPORTANT: A bug with gas estimation was found (it's free but still estimated), which causes issues with some nodes when a claim transaction is sent from api node. Please follow this guide for troubleshooting: https://discord.com/channels/1336477374442770503/1408382697537011814/1448254396218605721

messages.excerpt:
Hello team,
I've successfully set up my Gonka node and it's ready to participate in the network. However, I'm currently stuck at the grant-ml-ops-permissions step — the transaction requires gas fees, but I don't have any GNK tokens yet.
Without the ML permissions, my node cannot start mining and contribute to the network.
Could you please send some GNK tokens to my account?
Account Key: gonka1hq779w7kzr52edpvyu49tz6tghaarhf4v3wjvv
I would really appreciate your help. Thank you for your time!

./inferenced tx inference claim-rewards \
8033230422378114177 113 \

messages.text:
@BerlinBro,Help me out:
I have 3 unlaimed rewards,
one wallet is :
gonka1ujnc662v6g69jm6fgxnr79a2m7ehzeut059239
(1) I upgraded decentralized-api binary manually inside API container directory using:
wget -q -O decentralized-api.zip https://github.com/product-science/race-releases/releases/download/release%2Fv0.2.5-post7/decentralized-api-amd64.zip
sudo unzip -o -j decentralized-api.zip -d .dapi/cosmovisor/genesis/bin/
sudo chmod +x .dapi/cosmovisor/genesis/bin/decentralized-api
Container restarted correctly. Node stayed synced.
(2) I executed both claim methods successfully:
First:
POST http://localhost:9200/admin/v1/claim-reward/recover
with body {“force_claim”: true, “epoch_id”: 114}
Response returned:
{“success”:true,“epoch_index”:114,“seed”:8181541992154962346,“already_claimed”:true,“claim_executed”:true}
Second (chain tx):
./inferenced tx inference claim-rewards 8181541992154962346 114 –from gonka-account-key –keyring-backend file –chain-id gonka-mainnet –node http://node2.gonka.ai:8000/chain-rpc/ -y
tx returned success with hash:
0CB5FB036F237BDDDDFDB2DB044917D23244548817592DE02E2C61C54D1AE382
(3) But both chain query + dashboard still show epoch 114 unclaimed:
using chain-api:
“…/epoch_performance_summary/114/”
still returns “claimed”: false
dashboard also continues showing epoch 114 = Unclaimed instead of Claimed.
My value for reward_coins for epoch 114 is:
34 GNK
Questions:
1. Is this expected behaviour, i.e. rewards become visible only after next PoC/epoch cycle?
2. If not, what further action should I take to finalize claim?
3. Can the team confirm whether epoch 114 claim has been successfully processed or still pending internally?

Hi @Host ! A few nodes were submitting nonces, but the submitted values were invalid.
During the recent upgrade, the chain updated r_target from 1.401 to 1.398 .
Current target value:
curl "http://node1.gonka.ai:8000/chain-api/productscience/inference/inference/params" | jq '.params.poc_params.model_params.r_target'
Based on the observed nonces, a few nodes appear to be operating with the previous r_target value. This may indicate that the API container is still using an outdated binary.
Check the list below if your node falls under this case and should be checked.

messages.excerpt:
Hi @Host, check whether your node is on the list. If so, there are two possible cases for the previous epoch (121):
— If no reward was assigned, this indicates that something is not working correctly and your node configuration should be checked.
— If a reward was assigned but not claimed, make sure your node claims it before the next epoch starts.

messages.excerpt:
Another address
http://node1.gonka.ai:8000/dashboard/gonka/account/gonka1jqlwkd708ths3fzrz7mga7uuv40esryaf09y77
I saved seeds before turn off the node. Tried to force claim reward with 123 seed and 124 seed, but no effect
What to do?
inferenced tx inference claim-rewards \
<SEED> <EPOCH_ID> \
--from <KEY_NAME> \
--gas auto \
--gas-adjustment 1.3 \
--keyring-backend file \
--chain-id gonka-mainnet \
--node http://gonka.spv.re:8000/chain-rpc/

messages.excerpt:
My node gonka1pvkv59e72vju2h7s9j3ex62c5xneqey350vpwn is registered and shows ACTIVE on dashboard (weight 2917), but I'm not in epoch 127 participants. 
curl localhost:8000/v1/epochs/current/participants
returns null for my address. 
All inferences show ShouldValidate:false. Registered during epoch 126, now epoch 127 started but still no work assigned. What's blocking my node from joining the epoch?

messages.excerpt:
Hi @Host , check whether your node is on the list. If so, there are two possible cases for the previous epoch (126):
— If no reward was assigned, this indicates that something is not working correctly, and your node configuration should be checked.
— If a reward was assigned but not claimed, make sure your node claims it before the next epoch starts.
@Sergey_us @votkon @Nikita @mus_aushev

messages.text:
Run the AUTHZ GRANT command
On the node, run exactly this (single command):
docker exec -it node inferenced tx authz grant \
gonka1d7740l5ac4xnwkyfd44aevtkfvnctae6akw7jf \
generic \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgStartInference" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgFinishInference" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgClaimRewards" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgValidation" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgSubmitPocBatch" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgSubmitPocValidation" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgSubmitSeed" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgBridgeExchange" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgSubmitTrainingKvRecord" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgJoinTraining" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgJoinTrainingStatus" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgTrainingHeartbeat" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgSetBarrier" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgClaimTrainingTaskForAssignment" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgAssignTrainingTask" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgSubmitNewUnfundedParticipant" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgSubmitHardwareDiff" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgInvalidateInference" \
--msg-type "/inference.inference.MsgRevalidateInference" \
--msg-type "/inference.bls.MsgSubmitDealerPart" \
--msg-type "/inference.bls.MsgSubmitVerificationVector" \
--msg-type "/inference.bls.MsgRequestThresholdSignature" \
--msg-type "/inference.bls.MsgSubmitPartialSignature" \
--msg-type "/inference.bls.MsgSubmitGroupKeyValidationSignature" \
--from <COLD_KEY_NAME> \
--chain-id gonka-mainnet-1 \
--gas auto --gas-adjustment 1.5
<COLD_KEY_NAME> is the name in your keyring (not the address).
Restart API (important)
docker restart api
Verify it worked
Re-run:
curl -s http://localhost:9200/admin/v1/setup/report | jq '.[] | .checks[] | select(.id=="permissions_granted")' (edited)

wget -q -O in...
messages.text:
Thanks and Happy New Year!
Tania Charchian, [1/3/2026 7:00 AM]
Hi! There is a new version of the Node container: https://github.com/gonka-ai/gonka/pull/515
It should help with the network spam that has been happening over the last couple of days.
It's safe to do this when no Confirmation PoC is running. To check if Confirmation PoC is active:
curl "http://136.243.34.19:8000/v1/epochs/latest" | jq '.is_confirmation_poc_active'
This should return "false".
Get a new binary and restart the container:
Download Binarysudo rm -rf inferenced.zip .inference/cosmovisor/upgrades/v0.2.6-ante6/ .inference/data/upgrade-info.json
sudo mkdir -p .inference/cosmovisor/upgrades/v0.2.6-ante6/bin/
wget -q -O inferenced.zip 'https://github.com/product-science/race-releases/releases/download/release%2Fv0.2.6-ante6/inferenced-amd64.zip' && \
echo "2ab299313384370c638c37db82d91c3977dd2de4f57578d873c03fc9d29f664e inferenced.zip" | sha256sum --check && \
sudo unzip -o -j inferenced.zip -d .inference/cosmovisor/upgrades/v0.2.6-ante6/bin/ && \
sudo chmod +x .inference/cosmovisor/upgrades/v0.2.6-ante6/bin/inferenced && \
echo "Inference Installed and Verified"
Link Binaryecho "--- Final Verification ---" && \
sudo rm -rf .inference/cosmovisor/current
sudo ln -sf upgrades/v0.2.6-ante6 .inference/cosmovisor/current
echo "3e5b0d57683998b51d559bc0a58eefd7fd6a83d8bc934b202d248613a2c10030 .inference/cosmovisor/current/bin/inferenced" | sudo sha256sum --check && \
Restart source config.env && docker compose up node --no-deps --force-recreate -d
Thank you!

messages.text:
Hi team, I’m setting up a new Gonka host and my network node is running, but I can’t pull the MLNode image needed for inference / PoC. 
EnvironmentOS: Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS GPU: 2 × RTX 5090, CUDA 13.0 Network node images (all pulled and running): ghcr.io/product-science/inferenced:0.2.7-post1 ghcr.io/product-science/api:0.2.7-post1 ghcr.io/product-science/tmkms-softsign-with-keygen:0.2.7-post1 ghcr.io/product-science/bridge:0.2.5-post5 ghcr.io/product-science/proxy:0.2.7-post1 
Issue
When I try to pull or start MLNode, I get manifest unknown:
docker pull ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:latest
# or via docker compose with:
# image: ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:latest
# Result:
# Error response from daemon: manifest unknown
So the inference container fails to start (compose shows ✘ inference Error manifest unknown), while other services (node, api, proxy, bridge, tmkms, explorer) are all up and healthy.
Questions
1. What is the correct public image and tag for MLNode that hosts should use right now? 
e.g. something like ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:<version> instead of latest? 2. Do I need to authenticate to ghcr (e.g. docker login ghcr.io with a GitHub token) to pull the MLNode image, or should it be publicly pullable? 
3. If MLNode images are not yet available for public hosts, is there an ETA / version I should wait for?
My host wallet address: gonka1e37hxz3gj4f8nxfhn4n2e889pvrvqxn2as9cty.
Thanks!

Hi I'm on my way to setup new node, but I keep on getting error logs with 
node | + output='Usage:
node | inferenced download-genesis [node-address] [output-file] [flags]
node | 
node | Flags:
node | -h, --help help for download-genesis
node | 
node | Global Flags:
node | --home string directory for config and data (default "/root/.inference")
node | --log_format string The logging format (json|plain) (default "plain")
node | --log_level string The logging level (trace|debug|info|warn|error|fatal|panic|disabled or '"'"'*:<level>,<key>:<level>'"'"') (default "info")
node | --log_no_color Disable colored logs
node | --trace print out full stack trace on errors
node | 
node | failed to download genesis: failed to download genesis Get "http://node2.gonka.ai:26657/genesis": dial tcp 195.242.13.239:26657: connect: connection refused'
Is there anyway to fix this? (edited)

messages.text:
Validator completely removed from staking module — cannot recover
Timeline:Yesterday: node running normally on shared server (65.108.33.100), already on v0.2.9Today before epoch end: started getting jailedBegan migrating architecture — adding dedicated server to reduce loadDuring migration, validator was completely removed from staking module
Current state: query staking validator returns "validator not found". Participant exists in inference module (/v1/participants/ returns pubkey), but absent from staking module and /v1/epochs/current/participants.
Recovery attempts (all failed to restore staking entry):
1. unjail → "validator does not exist"
2. register-new-participant from API container → success, URL updated
3. tx inference submit-new-participant on-chain (tx 523C64AD...D8F89A) → code 0
4. None of these restored the staking module record
Node fully synced, all containers running, all keys preserved (Account Key, ML Operational Key, Consensus Key/TMKMS).
Question: How to restore the validator in the staking module? Is there a special command or does this need team intervention?

messages.author: Leo
messages.caveat: Discord excerpt is searchable context.
messages.excerpt:
Здравствуйте, у меня такой вопрос: я изначально арендовал машину наа vast.ai. Там по умолчанию на машинах не открыает указанные порты, а пробрасывает их по NAT на случайные внешние. Так порты 8000, 5000, 26657 извне закрыты и есть другой набор портов, которые передадресуются на указанные. Допустим у меня 53315 -> 8000. Проблема в том, что по всей видимости, я изначально зарегистрировал ноду с адресом, в котором был указан неправильный порт: 
82.79.4.199:8000
при помощи команды 
./inferenced register-new-participant "$DAPI_APIPUBLIC_URL" "$ACCOUNT_PUBKEY" \
--node-address "$SEED_API_URL" 
Выполнил её внутри контейнера api. Реальный порт, который переадресует на 8000 другой - 53315. Я поменял...
messages.text:
Здравствуйте, у меня такой вопрос: я изначально арендовал машину наа vast.ai. Там по умолчанию на машинах не открыает указанные порты, а пробрасывает их по NAT на случайные внешние. Так порты 8000, 5000, 26657 извне закрыты и есть другой набор портов, которые передадресуются на указанные. Допустим у меня 53315 -> 8000. Проблема в том, что по всей видимости, я изначально зарегистрировал ноду с адресом, в котором был указан неправильный порт: 
82.79.4.199:8000
при помощи команды 
./inferenced register-new-participant "$DAPI_APIPUBLIC_URL" "$ACCOUNT_PUBKEY" \
--node-address "$SEED_API_URL" 
Выполнил её внутри контейнера api. Реальный порт, который переадресует на 8000 другой - 53315. Я поменял PUBLIC_URL в config.env, перезапустил контейнеры. Вижу что значение переменной DAPI_APIPUBLIC_URL в нём изменилось - теперь указывает адрес с верным портом. 
Запрос
curl -s http://82.79.4.199:53315/health
возвращает "healthy"
ч
Перед началом новой эпохи (часов за 5) повторил команду ./inferenced register-new-participant, чтобы обновить регистрационные данные (именно адрес с верным портом). Дождался вчера новой эпохи (полагая, что эти данные кэшируются на время эпохи). Проверяю какой адрес отдаёт сеть командой:
curl -s "http://node2.gonka.ai:8000/v1/epochs/current/participants" \
| jq -r '.active_participants.participants[] | select(.index=="'"$COLD_ADDR"'") | .inference_url'
Однако в ответ получаю адрес с прежним портом
82.79.4.199:8000
И полагаю, что на ноду для выполнения полезной работы диспетчер сети прислать задачу не сможет, потому что, судя по-всему, у него остался адрес с неправильно указанным портом. Что я делаю не так? Или я не туда смотрю? Я вижу свой холодный адрес в списке валидаторов, но не вижу в контейнере запросов на выполнение полезной работы модели как хост. Подскажите, пожалуйста, решение (edited)

Подскажите плиз, в чем может быть проблема? (edited)

messages.excerpt:
Всем привет.
Вчера накосячил со сменой консенсус-ключа и улетел в джейл. Сейчас, после старта новой эпохи, репорт от АПИ на нетворк-ноде выдает PASS по всем проверкам, кроме наличия валидатора в сэте, а статус валидатора в дашборде - undefined. При этом МЛ-нода с некоторой периодичностью получает реквесты и не простаивает, как в прошлую эпоху.
Буду ли я завтра возвращен в сет автоматически, или надо еще что сделать?
Account Address: gonka1a7yqal0cdz0j5ehwnufrnd82q8fuvqk9sqnvtw

Здравствуйте. Vast.ai делает перенаправление портов, например
Open Ports:
11.22.33.44:34070 -> 26657/tcp
11.22.33.44:33706 -> 5000/tcp
11.22.33.44:20411 -> 8000/tcp
Поэтому сервер не будет доступен извне на этих портах. Это проблема? Может кто-то подсказать, если нужно эти внешние порты указать где-то в конфиге? Спасибо!

messages.excerpt:
Пытаюсь запустить ML-node. Возникает ошибка при выполнении команды привязки ML-key к аккаунту.
Команда, которую запускаю в WSL:
./inferenced tx inference grant-ml-ops-permissions \
gonka_account \
gonka1jz47j2pc7up7w840udak0vyzs995zgc6nsnh6t \
--from gonka_account \
--keyring-backend file \
--gas 2000000 \
--node http://node2.gonka.ai:26657
Ответ сети:
failed to get account details: rpc error: code = NotFound desc = account gonka16c4q44hlm8zkzhv420tf72zffctgzmgxpnpkg5 not found: key not found
Нода сама по себе запускается (node, tmkms работают), но транзакция не проходит.
Подскажите, в чём проблема? Нужно ли предварительно активировать аккаунт каким-то образом?

Всем привет из HOT Labs! 
Я хочу задеплоить смарт контракт и дернуть его стейт через RPC. Я умею это делать с другими чейнами на Cosmos sdk: через rest, либо через gRPC ( cosmwasm.wasm.v1.Query сервис).
Я чекнул код на гитхабе: wasm keeper реализуется и вместе с ним торчат наружу нужные интерфейсы. 
Вопрос: где я могу найти RPC, которые экспоузят cosm wasm интерфейс? 
В квикгайде вижу адреса из генезиса 
https://gonka.ai/developer/quickstart/#3-inference-using-modified-openai-sdk
Но по ним ничего не получается простучать. 
Посмотрел ip активных участников 
curl http://47.236.26.199:8000/chain-rpc/net_info | jq . 
по ним тоже не получилось(

messages.excerpt:
Привет всем,
Столкнулся с серьезной проблемой на моей ноде Gonka, интересно, может кто-то еще сталкивался с чем-то подобным.
Проблема в том, что контейнер ноды постоянно крашится с ошибкой "fatal error: too many concurrent timer firings" -
это ошибка Go runtime. Что особенно беспокоит - крашы становятся все более частыми.
Вот хронология:
30 ноября, 04:51 UTC - краш после 37 часов 56 минут работы30 ноября, 14:51 UTC - краш после всего 6 часов 14 минут (через 10 часов)30 ноября, 21:07 UTC - краш после 3 часов 40 минут (через 6 часов)1 декабря, 02:44 UTC - краш после всего 2 часов 34 минут (через 5.5 часов)
То есть время работы стремительно падает: 37.9 часов, потом 6.2 часа, потом 3.7 часа, п...
messages.text:
Привет всем,
Столкнулся с серьезной проблемой на моей ноде Gonka, интересно, может кто-то еще сталкивался с чем-то подобным.
Проблема в том, что контейнер ноды постоянно крашится с ошибкой "fatal error: too many concurrent timer firings" -
это ошибка Go runtime. Что особенно беспокоит - крашы становятся все более частыми.
Вот хронология:
30 ноября, 04:51 UTC - краш после 37 часов 56 минут работы30 ноября, 14:51 UTC - краш после всего 6 часов 14 минут (через 10 часов)30 ноября, 21:07 UTC - краш после 3 часов 40 минут (через 6 часов)1 декабря, 02:44 UTC - краш после всего 2 часов 34 минут (через 5.5 часов)
То есть время работы стремительно падает: 37.9 часов, потом 6.2 часа, потом 3.7 часа, потом 2.6 часа. Каждый крашпроисходит быстрее предыдущего.
Паттерн перед каждым крашем одинаковый - куча ошибок PoC от разных участников с "Failed to get upcoming epoch",
затем срабатывает fatal error с таймерами и контейнер перезапускается.
У меня все хорошо с железом - NVIDIA H100, 118GB оперативки, достаточно места на дисках, все 8 контейнеров
работают нормально. Это точно не проблема нехватки памяти. Нода сразу синхронизируется после каждого перезапуска и
майнинг продолжается, но растущая частота крашей реально беспокоит.
Я исключил проблемы с инфраструктурой, дисками, ручным вмешательством - с моей стороны все чисто. Похоже, это
что-то в самом коде Gonka node, конкретно связанное с управлением таймерами в Go runtime.
Мои вопросы:
Кто-нибудь еще видит такое?Это известная проблема?Есть какие-то воркэраунды или фиксы на подходе?Стоит ли настроить превентивные перезапуски каждые пару часов?
Нода продолжает майнить между крашами, так как Docker автоматически ее перезапускает, но если тренд продолжится,скоро буду получать крашы каждый час или чаще. Был бы очень признателен за любые советы или подтверждение, что
другие тоже это видят.
Адрес участника: gonka10az8z6jv7lkywkjeajah2sdd9c0ysdm3rc09j0
Заранее спасибо за любую помощь.

Проблема в том, что синхронизация идёт уже ~8 часов, и при этом она очень медленная:
catching_up: true
скорость по замеру: ~19 blocks/min (delta=19 blocks/min)
отставание от seed: было порядка ~26k блоков (seed выше)
По пирам:
n_peers: 8, но INBOUND = 0 (в net_info все is_outbound:true, строк is_outbound:false нет). Порт 5000 на хосте слушается, INPUT policy ACCEPT (iptables), ufw не видно активным, но inbound всё равно не появляется.
По диску:
основная масса занята в ~/gonka/deploy/join/.inference/data — около 704 GB.
после очистки кэшей свободно...
messages.text:
Сейчас node (Cosmos/Tendermint) поднялся и работает, P2P слушает и правильно анонсируется как tcp://109.248.46.153:5000, RPC доступен на :26657. 
Проблема в том, что синхронизация идёт уже ~8 часов, и при этом она очень медленная:
catching_up: true
скорость по замеру: ~19 blocks/min (delta=19 blocks/min)
отставание от seed: было порядка ~26k блоков (seed выше)
По пирам:
n_peers: 8, но INBOUND = 0 (в net_info все is_outbound:true, строк is_outbound:false нет). Порт 5000 на хосте слушается, INPUT policy ACCEPT (iptables), ufw не видно активным, но inbound всё равно не появляется.
По диску:
основная масса занята в ~/gonka/deploy/join/.inference/data — около 704 GB.
после очистки кэшей свободно стало ~132 GB, use ~87% — всё ещё тесно, база продолжает расти.
В логах node встречается:
ERR panic recovered in runTx ... out of gas in location: ReadPerByte ...
и сообщения от Claims:
Validate request failed error="No rewards for this address" ... module=x/inference subsystem=Claims (для двух адресов)
GPT предлагает решение (чтобы резко ускорить догон и освободить сотни GB):
сделать reset только blockstore/state (не трогая keyring/config/eth-часть), то есть удалить:
.inference/data/blockstore.db
.inference/data/state.db
затем перезапустить node — он заново поднимется и догонит сеть через state sync/быстрый режим, вместо многосуточного “полного” догоняющего блока.
Вопросы:
Норма ли, что INBOUND = 0 при открытом :5000, и что у других появляется is_outbound:false?
Подтвердите, пожалуйста: рекомендуемый подход — “сбрасывать blockstore/state и синкаться через state sync”, а не тянуть full sync (который уже идёт 8 часов и даёт ~19 blocks/min)?
Кто-то видел ошибки вида panic recovered ... out of gas in ReadPerByte и влияет ли это на скорость синка/награды?

messages.excerpt:
Валидатор полностью удалён из стейкинг-модуля — не могу разджейлить или пересоздать
Проблема: query staking validator возвращает "validator not found". unjail падает с ошибкой "validator does not exist". Валидатор был в jail 5 раз на shared сервере, вероятно удалён при обновлении v0.2.9 (24 участника убраны из allowlist).
Что я сделал:
Перенёс ноду на выделенный сервер (65.21.20.160)
Нода полностью синхронизирована, TMKMS подключен
register-new-participant из API контейнера — успешно, URL обновлён
tx inference submit-new-participant on-chain (tx 5EA02ED8DF316807F1E08616DC6DC7FFCECF69D1E36F55AD1A26DD55B9D47C73) — code 0, успешно
Участник существует в /v1/participants/
Но валидатора по-прежне...

5. Upgrade the ML Node image (required for PoC v2)
Edit docker-compose.mlnode.yml and update the ML Node image:
Standard GPUs
image: ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:3.0.12
NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs
image: ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:3.0.12-blackwell
Apply changes and restart services. From gonka/deploy/join:
source config.env
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.mlnode.yml pull
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.mlnode.yml up -d
6....
messages.text:
Note:node-config.json is used only on the first launch of the Network Node API or when the local state/database is removed. Edit it for a fresh restart. For existing nodes, model updates should be performed via the Admin API. 
5. Upgrade the ML Node image (required for PoC v2)
Edit docker-compose.mlnode.yml and update the ML Node image:
Standard GPUs
image: ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:3.0.12
NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs
image: ghcr.io/product-science/mlnode:3.0.12-blackwell
Apply changes and restart services. From gonka/deploy/join:
source config.env
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.mlnode.yml pull
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.mlnode.yml up -d
6. Verify model serving (applied at the next epoch)
Confirm the ML Node is serving Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8 only, which is the only model used for PoC v2 weights and future weight assignment:
curl http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/models | jq
Optionally re-check node allocation:
curl http://127.0.0.1:9200/admin/v1/nodes
Note: Governance and PoC v2 activation notes
PoC v2 is introduced in stages, not activated all at once.
Stage 1. Observation (current state after v0.2.8)
After the v0.2.8 upgrade, PoC v2 logic is available but not active for weight assignment.
During this stage:
Hosts are able to serve Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8 or Qwen/Qwen3-32B-FP8Hosts must switch their ML Nodes to serve Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8 and upgrade them to PoC v2-compatible versions in order to contribute to PoC v2 weight.The network observes adoption to assess Host readiness for moving to PoC v2 weights.
Stage 2. Governance proposal (optional, future)
Once a sufficient level of adoption among active Hosts is observed (approximately 50%):
A separate governance proposal may be submittedThis proposal may request approval to activate PoC v2 and use PoC v2 for weight assignmentThe adoption threshold is observational only and does not trigger any automatic changes.
Stage 3. Activation (only after governance approval)
PoC v2 becomes the active method of weight assignment only if and when the governance proposal is approved by the chain.
Until this proposal is approved:
PoC v2 remains inactive for weight assignmentThe existing PoC mechanism continues to be used to determine weight
Summary checklistBefore PoC v2 activation, ensure that:
ML Node serves Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8All other models are removed from the configurationML Node image is 3.0.12 (or 3.0.12-blackwell)

Script in the repository: 
BashPython.
ADMIN=${ADMIN:-http://127.0.0.1:9200}
KEEP=${KEEP:-Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8}
APPLY=${APPLY:-"--apply"}
curl -sS "$ADMIN/admin/v1/config" | jq -r --arg k "$KEEP" '
.nodes[] | "\(.id): " + (
if (.models | has($k) | not) then "skip (\(.models | keys))"
elif (.models | length) == 1 then "ok"
else "\(.models | keys) -> [\($k)]" end)'
if [[ "$APPLY" == "--apply" ]]; then
curl -sS "$ADMIN/admin/v1/config" \
| jq -c --arg k "$KEEP" \
'.nodes[] | select((.models | has($k)) and (.models | length > 1)) | .models = {($k): .models[$k]}' \
| while IFS= read -r p; do
id=$(jq -r .id <<<"$p")
curl -sS -f -X PUT -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$p"...
messages.text:
Script in the repository: 
BashPython.
ADMIN=${ADMIN:-http://127.0.0.1:9200}
KEEP=${KEEP:-Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8}
APPLY=${APPLY:-"--apply"}
curl -sS "$ADMIN/admin/v1/config" | jq -r --arg k "$KEEP" '
.nodes[] | "\(.id): " + (
if (.models | has($k) | not) then "skip (\(.models | keys))"
elif (.models | length) == 1 then "ok"
else "\(.models | keys) -> [\($k)]" end)'
if [[ "$APPLY" == "--apply" ]]; then
curl -sS "$ADMIN/admin/v1/config" \
| jq -c --arg k "$KEEP" \
'.nodes[] | select((.models | has($k)) and (.models | length > 1)) | .models = {($k): .models[$k]}' \
| while IFS= read -r p; do
id=$(jq -r .id <<<"$p")
curl -sS -f -X PUT -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$p" \
"$ADMIN/admin/v1/nodes/$id" >/dev/null && echo "$id: updated"
done
echo "done; persisted within 60s"
else
echo "preview only; rerun without APPLY=dry to commit"
fi
Wait 60 seconds after running the script to ensure the changes are persisted before triggering the upgrade. Then, verify the configuration:
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:9200/admin/v1/config \
| jq '.nodes[] | {id, models: (.models | keys)}'
Expected output:
{
"id": "<nodeId>",
"models": [
"Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8"
]
}
(Additional nodes will follow the same format)
@everyone
